Description: Checks whether at least one emergency access (break-glass) account exists in the tenant. These are cloud-only Global Administrator accounts excluded from Conditional Access policies, intended for use when normal admin access fails (e.g., MFA outage, federation failure).
Risk: Without break-glass accounts, an MFA service outage, misconfigured Conditional Access policy, or federated identity provider failure can lock all administrators out of the tenant. Recovery requires a Microsoft support ticket and can take days.

Remediation.Recommendation: Create at least two cloud-only break-glass accounts with Global Administrator role. Exclude them from all Conditional Access policies. Use long, complex passwords stored in a physical safe. Monitor sign-in activity on these accounts with alerts.
